title: Pull-Down Assay to Assess ATG2A Binding to WIPI4 and LC3B
author: Elisabeth Holzer

steps.title: Incubate beads coated with 3×FLAG-tagged ATG2A...
steps.instruction: Incubate beads coated with 3×FLAG-tagged ATG2A ( 5 µL slurry per condition) with 5 micromolar (µM) purified 6×HIS-tagged WIPI4, LC3B, or GFP (negative control) in SEC300 buffer.

steps.title: Incubate for 01:00:00 at room temperature with gentle rotation
steps.instruction: Incubate for 01:00:00 at room temperature with gentle rotation.

steps.title: Wash once with 100 µL SEC300 buffer
steps.instruction: Wash once with 100 µL SEC300 buffer. Add 40 µL SEC300 buffer to the beads.

steps.title: Split the sample into two equal parts
steps.instruction: Split the sample into two equal parts: Low-salt fraction – store on ice for analysis (see step 6) High-salt fraction

steps.title: For the high-salt fraction perform the...
steps.instruction: For the high-salt fraction perform the following washes ( 100 µL each): 2× washes with SEC300 buffer 1× wash with SEC500 buffer 2× washes with SEC300 buffer

steps.title: Resuspend beads of low- and high-salt...
steps.instruction: Resuspend beads of low- and high-salt fraction in 20 µL SEC300 and mix with SDS loading buffer.

steps.title: Analyze collected fractions by Western blot
steps.instruction: Analyze collected fractions by Western blot. Detect ATG2A ( anti-ATG2A Proteintech Catalog #23226-1-AP (RRID: AB_2879235) ) and HIS-tagged WIPI4 ( anti-HIS Qiagen Catalog #34660 (RRID: AB_2619735) ) using specific antibodies.
